parent.label: Multilingual
parent.description: Parallel corpora and multilingual text collections — documents that exist in multiple languages simultaneously. These are essential for training multilingual models because they provide alignment signal: the same meaning expressed in different linguistic forms. European Union documents are among the largest high-quality parallel corpora in the world.
bullets: Europarl: EU Parliament proceedings in 24 official languages, aligned sentence-by-sentence
CC-100: CommonCrawl filtered for 100 languages, unevenly quality-controlled
OPUS: aggregation of 90+ parallel translation corpora

cells.label: Europarl / EU Docs
cells.description: European Parliament proceedings translated into all 24 EU official languages, sentence-aligned. One of the highest-quality parallel corpora in existence: professional human translators, consistent subject matter, decades of coverage. Essential for multilingual model training.
bullets: 60M+ sentences in 24 languages, professionally translated by EU linguists
Sentence-aligned: the same speech in French, German, Polish, Bulgarian — simultaneously
Subject matter: law, regulation, debate — formal register across all languages
cells.screenshotCaption: European Parliament — proceedings published in 24 official languages, professionally translated

cells.label: CC-100 / Other
cells.description: CommonCrawl filtered for 100 languages using language identification — CC-100 (Facebook AI Research) and similar multilingual web corpora. Coverage is uneven: high-resource languages like French and Spanish have billions of tokens; low-resource languages may have only millions.
bullets: CC-100: 2.5TB of filtered text across 100 languages, widely used for multilingual pretraining
Language distribution is highly unequal — English is 55%+ of indexed web content
Quality varies dramatically by language; some languages have mostly machine-translated content
